title: IonQ | IonQ and ARLIS Partner to Secure Mission-Critical Quantum Systems
why:
- IonQ and ARLIS have announced a collaboration to develop a Zero Trust Security Framework for mission-critical quantum architectures, aiming to secure quantum computing systems.
- The initiative leverages NIST standards (specifically SP800-207) to shift quantum security from static perimeters to continuous verification across hardware, software, and networking ecosystems.
- The project, called SEQCURE, is supported by the Secretary of the Air Force’s Concepts, Development, and Management Office, highlighting its significance for national infrastructure.
- IonQ will help define ZTA (Zero Trust Architecture) standards to ensure secure deployment of quantum technologies across hardware, software, data, and cloud environments, with guidance for trusted integration across federal agencies.
- This collaboration builds on IonQ’s existing federal partnerships, including contracts with ARLIS, DARPA, and the U.S. Air Force Research Lab, reinforcing the company's leadership in secure quantum platform development.
